annotations.label: Binomial series

anchor.snippet: the binomial series is a generalization of the binomial formula

annotations.note: `binomialSeries` is the scalar formal multilinear series with coefficients `Ring.choose a`, directly formalizing the binomial series.

annotations.label: Generalized binomial coefficients

anchor.snippet: expressed in terms of the (generalized) binomial coefficients

annotations.note: `Ring.choose r n` generalizes `Nat.choose` via descending Pochhammer over `n!`, exactly the generalized binomial coefficient.

annotations.label: Binomial series as MacLaurin series

anchor.snippet: The binomial series is the MacLaurin series for the function

annotations.note: This states `(1+x)^a` has `binomialSeries ℂ a` as its power-series (Maclaurin) expansion on the unit ball (real version `Real.one_add_rpow_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero`).

annotations.label: Nonnegative integer exponent gives finite polynomial

anchor.snippet: all later terms in the series are 0

annotations.note: Radius `⊤` for natural `a` reflects finiteness, and `Ring.choose_natCast` plus `Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt` give the vanishing of later terms, but no single lemma states the series reduces to the finite polynomial.

annotations.label: Conditions for convergence

anchor.snippet: converges depends on the values of the complex numbers

annotations.note: Radius of convergence is `1` for non-natural `a` and `⊤` for natural `a` (`binomialSeries_radius_eq_top_of_nat`), determining convergence on the open disk.

annotations.label: Behavior on the boundary |x| = 1

anchor.snippet: the situation at the boundary of the disk of convergence

annotations.note: The detailed boundary-convergence classification depending on Re(α) and the points x = ±1 is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binomial coefficient identities

anchor.section: Identities to be used in the proof
anchor.snippet: The following hold for any complex number

annotations.note: Pascal's recurrence (`Ring.choose_succ_succ`) and Chu–Vandermonde (`Ring.add_choose_eq`) are formalized, but not the full collection of identities (e.g. `k·C(α,k)=α·C(α−1,k−1)`) used in the article's proof.

annotations.label: Asymptotic relationship for binomial coefficients

anchor.section: Identities to be used in the proof
anchor.snippet: a useful asymptotic relationship for the binomial coefficients is, in Landau notation

annotations.note: The asymptotic estimate for `C(α,k)` in terms of `k^{-(1+α)}/Γ(-α)` is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence with Euler's Gamma definition

anchor.section: Identities to be used in the proof
anchor.snippet: Euler's definition of the Gamma function

annotations.note: `Complex.GammaSeq_tendsto_Gamma` proves the Euler/Gauss limit `nˢ n!/(s(s+1)⋯(s+n)) → Γ(s)` (real version `Real.GammaSeq_tendsto_Gamma`).

annotations.label: Coarser bounds for the coefficients

anchor.section: Identities to be used in the proof
anchor.snippet: implies immediately the coarser bounds

annotations.note: Explicit growth/size bounds on the generalized binomial coefficients are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rewriting of the generalized binomial coefficient

anchor.section: Identities to be used in the proof
anchor.snippet: Formula ( 2 ) for the generalized binomial coefficient can be rewritten as

annotations.note: `Ring.choose_eq_smul` rewrites `choose a n = (n!)⁻¹ • (descPochhammer ℤ n).smeval a`, but the Gamma-function rewriting in the article is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sum solves an ODE

anchor.snippet: the sum of the series is an analytic function solving the ordinary differential equation

annotations.note: Mathlib has the derivative machinery used in the proof but no statement that the binomial-series sum solves the ODE `(1+x)u' = αu`.

annotations.label: Sum of the binomial series

anchor.snippet: The unique solution of this problem is the function

annotations.note: The sum of the binomial series equals `(1+x)^a` on the unit ball, exactly `Complex.one_add_cpow_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero` (and `Real.one_add_rpow_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero`).

annotations.label: Extension to the boundary via Abel's theorem

anchor.snippet: as a consequence of Abel's theorem

annotations.note: Abel's limit theorem for power series is formalized in general, but it is not applied to extend the binomial series to the boundary.

annotations.label: Negative binomial series

anchor.snippet: the negative binomial series defined by the MacLaurin series for the function

annotations.note: `Complex.one_div_one_sub_cpow_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero` gives `1/(1-x)^a = Σ choose(a+n-1,n) xⁿ`, the negative binomial series (real version also present).

annotations.label: Multiset coefficient

anchor.snippet: which is written in terms of the multiset coefficient

annotations.note: `Ring.multichoose r n` is the multiset coefficient (choosing with replacement), with `Ring.multichoose_eq` relating it to `choose (r+n-1) n`.

annotations.label: Case α = 1

anchor.snippet: The case α = 1 gives the series

annotations.note: `1/(1-x) = Σ xⁿ` is `Complex.one_div_one_sub_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero` (with `.ofScalars 1`), the α=1 case.

annotations.label: Case α = 2 (counting numbers)

anchor.snippet: The case α = 2 gives the series

annotations.note: `1/(1-x)^2 = Σ (n+1) xⁿ` is `Complex.one_div_one_sub_sq_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero`, exactly the α=2 case.

annotations.label: Case α = 3 (triangle numbers)

anchor.snippet: The case α = 3 gives the series

annotations.note: The general lemma `1/(1-x)^(a+1) = Σ choose(a+n,a) xⁿ` instantiated at `a=2` gives `1/(1-x)^3` with triangular-number coefficients.

annotations.label: Case α = 4 (tetrahedral numbers)

anchor.snippet: The case α = 4 gives the series

annotations.note: Instantiating the general lemma at `a=3` gives `1/(1-x)^4` with tetrahedral-number coefficients `choose(3+n,3)`.

annotations.label: Geometric series special case

anchor.snippet: The negative binomial series includes the case of the geometric series

annotations.note: The geometric series `1/(1-x) = Σ xⁿ` is the α=1 instance, formalized as `Complex.one_div_one_sub_hasFPowerSeriesOnBall_zero`.

anchors.snippet: The negative binomial series includes the case of the geometric series
